title: Chief executives and senior officials
description: Determine and formulate policies and provide overall direction of companies or private and public sector organizations within guidelines set up by a board of directors or similar governing body. Plan, direct, or coordinate operational activities at the highest level of management with the help of subordinate executives and staff managers. Communicate and coordinate with management, shareholders, customers, and employees to address sustainability issues. Enact or oversee a corporate sustainability strategy. Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. (Based on 18 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Develop and maintain sustainability program documents, such as schedules and budgets while ensuring compliance with environmental regulations.
Direct sustainability program operations to ensure compliance with environmental or governmental regulations and monitor effectiveness of sustainability programs.
Prepare and present reports concerning sustainability initiatives, activities, budgets, or other items affecting program services.
Formulate or implement policies related to fundraising programs and direct human resource activities, selecting or approving staff and establishing major departmental goals.
Confer with board members or organization officials to discuss issues, coordinate activities, or review policies and organizational effectiveness.
Develop budgetary control systems or administrative processes in conjunction with board members and ensure efficient funding for operations across departments.
Analyze operations and financial statements to evaluate performance against objectives and identify areas for improvement or cost reduction.
Identify educational or training opportunities for employees in sustainability and conduct evaluations of sustainability initiatives.
Keep informed of regulations affecting emergency plans and maintain liaisons with local governments and organizations for effective emergency management coordination.
Plan and direct special events for fundraising, coordinate marketing strategies for sustainability, and promote organizational objectives at official functions.
Monitor suppliers to ensure provision of needed goods within budgetary limits and negotiate contracts or agreements efficiently to support organizational goals.
Verify staff credentials, such as educational and certification requirements, and hire associates to fill vacancies.
Direct facility maintenance or repair and oversee internal reporting systems, such as corporate compliance hotlines.
Communicate regulatory information to multiple departments and ensure that employees are informed about compliance reporting systems, policies, and practices.
Maintain client databases and store and retrieve equipment, such as vehicles and radios.
Participate in continuing education classes to maintain current knowledge of industry and keep informed regarding pending changes, trends, or best practices.
Plan, organize, or lead group activities for customers and develop relationships with stakeholders to coordinate operational activities.
Formulate or implement regulatory affairs policies and procedures to ensure compliance and provide guidance to departments regarding marketing of products.
Direct the preparation and submission of regulatory applications, reports, or correspondence and monitor compliance systems to ensure effectiveness.
Evaluate regulatory affairs aspects related to sustainability and monitor trends regarding environmental issues impacting the organization.
Plan, draft, and review budgets to assist in strategic planning or operational initiatives and analyze retail data to identify trends.
Write and present strategies for compliance-related operations and provide employee training on compliance topics, policies, or procedures.
Prepare management reports regarding compliance operations and maintain documentation of compliance activities, such as complaints received or investigation outcomes.
Conduct internal investigations of compliance issues and serve as a confidential point of contact for employees to report irregularities.
Make recommendations and oversee improvements in corporate sustainability strategies and risk management strategies based on compliance assessments.
Negotiate contracts for services or materials needed for projects and coordinate on-site activities to ensure compliance with environmental laws or standards.
Develop processes or procedures for wind operations, including transitioning from construction to commercial operations.
Establish goals, objectives, or priorities for wind field operations and manage daily operations across multiple departments.
Prepare wind field operational budgets and submit budget estimates, progress reports, or cost tracking reports.
Review or evaluate proposals or bids to make recommendations regarding awarding contracts and negotiate tax agreements.
Supervise employees or subcontractors to ensure quality of work or adherence to safety regulations or policies.
Provide technical support for the design, construction, or commissioning of wind farm projects and prepare requests for proposals.
Monitor and maintain records of daily facility operations and create project status presentations for delivery to stakeholders.
Order parts, tools, or equipment needed to maintain, restore, or improve wind field operations and oversee the maintenance of associated equipment.
Review civil design, engineering, or construction technical documentation to ensure compliance with applicable codes and regulations.
Provide verbal or written project status reports to project teams, management, customers, or owners and identify project needs such as resources, staff, or finances.
